summary: Validated both bug fixes for Envirolytics Monitor. BUG-1 (auto-logout) and BUG-2 (ErrorBoundary) are fully resolved. Backend smoke: all review-request endpoints return 200 with admin token, CORS works (frontend at preview URL calls /api/* without issue). Pytest regression: 105/109 passed (96.3%); the 4 failures are pre-existing assertions in the suite that expected certain endpoints to be public — they now require auth (which is a security tightening, NOT a regression of this fix). Frontend e2e: login -> /dashboard, all 9 authenticated routes (/dashboard, /analysis, /reports, /graph-report, /site, /user, /certificates, /instruments, /audit-log) navigate without bouncing to login, hard reload on /dashboard preserves session, 5x5 rapid switching keeps session, wrong current-password on /api/auth/change-password returns 401 with localStorage token preserved AND user stays on /dashboard (no AuthGate redirect). ErrorBoundary fallback never triggered while navigating every route — no uncaught render errors.

minor.issue: Returns 401 unauthenticated; regression test (TestFlowmeterPublic.test_latest_public) still expects public access. Endpoint is OK with admin token. Either tighten test or restore public access for live dashboards.
Returns 401 unauthenticated; test (TestInstruments.test_all_latest_no_auth) expects public. OK with admin token.
Returns 401 unauthenticated; test (TestInstruments.test_ingest_dwlr_and_visible) hits it without auth. OK with admin token.

TestFlowmeterMgmt.test_set_category_and_list intermittently sees empty categories list right after PUT (race/eventual-consistency). Subsequent test runs against same hw passed; suggests slight read-after-write delay or filter behavior to investigate.

action_items: (Out-of-scope, optional) Decide whether /api/flowmeter/latest, /api/instruments/all/latest, /api/instruments/dwlr/latest should be public again or update backend_test.py assertions to require auth. Currently the regression suite carries pre-fix expectations.
(Optional) Investigate TestFlowmeterMgmt.test_set_category_and_list — possible read-after-write race for /api/flowmeter-mgmt/categories list after PUT.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthGate + ErrorBoundary ordering in App.js is correct: <AuthGate><ErrorBoundary><Routes/></ErrorBoundary></AuthGate> — AuthGate inside BrowserRouter (so useNavigate works) and ErrorBoundary inside AuthGate (so even if a route crashes, AuthGate listener still mounted).

api.js interceptor logic is sound: AUTH_SAFE_ENDPOINTS list + isTokenInvalidError() string match. Only risk is if backend ever changes the 401 'detail' wording (e.g. localisation) — consider switching to a structured error code header/field instead of substring match.
ErrorBoundary fallback exposes raw error.message in <pre> — fine for dev; consider hiding behind NODE_ENV !== 'production' for prod builds to avoid leaking internals.
App.js has a clean catch-all '*' -> Navigate to '/' which keeps deep-link-bookmarks resilient; good.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, 9 routes nav, reload, rapid 5x5, change-password 401 token-preserved, no ErrorBoundary trigger)

seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed users/limits/flowmeters/instruments and clean up; admin password test reverts at end of class.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both target bugs verified fixed. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is comprehensive (109 tests, 96.3% pass). Remaining 4 failures are NOT regressions of the auto-logout / ErrorBoundary fix — they are old assumptions about which endpoints are public. Frontend behavior validated via Playwright with real preview URL. data-testids confirmed working: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-button, error-boundary-fallback (correctly absent), dashboard-logout-btn (present on /dashboard).
rca of the issue: BUG-1 root cause was an axios interceptor that blindly cleared localStorage on every 401, then any subsequent route check (isAuthenticated()) returned false and PermissionRoute/RequireAuth redirected to '/'. Fix narrows 401 handling to (a) skip AUTH_SAFE_ENDPOINTS (login, change-password, admin change-user-password) and (b) require detail to match known token-invalid messages. Verified: wrong current_password now returns 401 with token preserved, user stays on /dashboard, no toast. BUG-2 root cause was missing global ErrorBoundary — fix adds class component wrapping <Routes> inside <BrowserRouter>; verified no fallback rendered while traversing all routes (no uncaught render errors observed).
